title: Comprehensive Health Service
principles: Use tsconfig path aliases (@/, @db/, @ai/, @logging/, @modules/) — never relative paths like '../../foo'
Every modular health.ts exports `checkHealth(env: Env): Promise<HealthStepResult>` — identical signature, no exceptions
Every check actually exercises the subsystem and validates response SHAPE — never `return { status: 'ok' }`
Use `safeRun` to wrap every sub-check; one crashing sub-check must not kill the suite
Run sub-checks in parallel with Promise.all when independent
Schema changes always: pnpm run db:generate. Never hand-edit drizzle/*.sql
wrangler.jsonc binding/var changes always: pnpm run cf-typegen immediately after
Add @callable() to all 4 agent classes; rename method to healthProbe; coordinator uses getAgentByName(env.X, 'health-probe').healthProbe()
Drive lifecycle retains the most recent run's docs so the user can manually verify URL rendering on the frontend
Frontend response shape changes from { screening: { resultsJson } } to { run, results } — update HealthDashboard and HealthBadge in lockstep with route changes

phases.name: Shared types + new schema

tasks.title: Create shared health types
actions: Create src/health/types.ts exporting HealthStatus, CheckStatus, HealthCategory, HealthStepResult, HealthRun, HealthResult per PRD §5
Extend HealthCategory enum with values 'binding', 'google', and keep existing values from cloudflare-jedi
Add a transition shim: `export type ModuleResult = HealthStepResult` so legacy callers compile until P6 cleanup
acceptance: src/health/types.ts compiles standalone (tsc --noEmit on the file)
All exported types match cloudflare-jedi references/health-system.md lines 50-86 verbatim

tasks.title: Create relational health schema
actions: runs.ts: define healthRuns table per PRD §4 (id text PK, status enum, trigger enum, duration_ms, created_at, metadata JSON). Export createSelectSchema + createInsertSchema
results.ts: define healthResults with FK to healthRuns (onDelete: 'cascade'). Category enum includes 'binding' and 'google'. Export selectHealthResultSchema
test-definitions.ts: define healthTestDefinitions per cloudflare-jedi reference
index.ts: barrel `export * from './runs'`, etc.
acceptance: All 3 tables defined with correct FK, default(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), and JSON modes
Drizzle-zod select+insert schemas exported

tasks.title: Wire health schema into db/schema.ts (transitional — keep health-screenings)
actions: Add `export * from './schemas/health'` to the schema barrel
Keep the existing health-screenings re-export — it gets removed in P6
acceptance: db/schema.ts re-exports both legacy health-screenings and new health/* schemas with no duplicate-export errors

tasks.title: Generate migration for the 3 new tables
actions: pnpm run db:generate
Inspect generated SQL — must add health_runs, health_results, health_test_definitions; must NOT touch health_screenings yet
acceptance: New file in drizzle/ adds the three tables
pnpm run db:migrate:local applies cleanly

verification: wrangler d1 execute core-resumes --local --command "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'" → includes health_runs, health_results, health_test_definitions, health_screenings

tasks.title: Add RESUME_TEMPLATE_DOC_ID to wrangler.jsonc vars
actions: Add `RESUME_TEMPLATE_DOC_ID: "<doc-id-here>"` under the `vars` block
Run `pnpm run cf-typegen` (or `wrangler types`)
Confirm Env interface in worker-configuration.d.ts now exposes RESUME_TEMPLATE_DOC_ID
acceptance: Env now includes RESUME_TEMPLATE_DOC_ID as string
User must populate the actual Doc ID before deploy — log this requirement in implementation log

phases.name: Modular health.ts files (one per subsystem)

tasks.title: src/backend/db/health.ts
actions: Export checkHealth(env): SELECT 1 + PRAGMA table_list
Assert key tables exist: roles, documents, threads, messages, health_runs, health_results
Return details: { tableCount, latencyMs, expectedTablesPresent: boolean, missingTables: string[] }
acceptance: Returns failure when a key table is missing
Returns success with details.tableCount > 0 when healthy

tasks.title: src/backend/ai/health.ts (Workers AI native)
actions: Sub-check 1: env.AI.run(env.DEFAULT_MODEL_EMBEDDING, { text: ['health'] }) — assert result.data is number[][] and data[0].length > 0
Sub-check 2: env.AI.run('@cf/meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct', { messages: [{role:'user', content:'Reply with the word Pong.'}], max_tokens: 10 }) — assert response is non-empty string
Run sub-checks via Promise.all + safeRun
acceptance: Empty embeddings array → failure
Empty chat response → failure
Healthy response on both → success with details containing both sub-check latencies

tasks.title: src/backend/ai/providers/health.ts (multi-provider via AI Gateway)
actions: Verify AI_GATEWAY_ACCOUNT_ID and AI_GATEWAY_GATEWAY_ID present at top — fail fast if missing
Probe workers-ai (always), openai/anthropic/google when their key is configured (else SKIPPED)
Each: generateText({ prompt: 'Reply with exactly the word: Pong', maxTokens: 10 }) — reject empty/non-string responses
Use the existing src/backend/ai/providers/index.ts createModel factory
acceptance: Missing gateway IDs → failure with explicit message
Unconfigured providers report SKIPPED with reason, not FAILURE
details exposes per-provider response, latency, provider

tasks.title: src/backend/ai/tools/google/health.ts (REPLACE existing — Drive + Docs lifecycle)
actions: Add GoogleDriveClient.copyFile(sourceFileId, name, parentFolderId) — POST /drive/v3/files/{id}/copy with {name, parents}
List PARENT_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID and HEALTH_CHECK_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID
Order children of HEALTH_CHECK_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID by modifiedTime desc; retain the most recent run's set; delete only older docs
Create doc from HTML via existing createDocFromHtml + renderDocumentTemplate('resume', ...)
Create doc from template via copyFile(env.RESUME_TEMPLATE_DOC_ID, ...)
Append marker via GoogleDocsClient.appendText, then getDocument and assert body contains the marker
Return details: { createdDocIds, createdDocUrls, folderUrls: { parent, health }, retainedPriorRunIds, deletedCount }
acceptance: Fresh second run deletes the first run's docs but keeps its own
createdDocUrls present in details and clickable from the frontend
appendText + getDocument round-trip confirms write persisted

tasks.title: Per-agent health.ts rewrite (4 agents)
actions: Each file exports BOTH `healthProbe(agent, env)` (deep work, called from inside the @callable) AND `checkHealth(env)` (coordinator-side bridge using getAgentByName + stub.healthProbe())
checkHealth bridge MUST capture probeMs by timing the stub.healthProbe() call
checkHealth bridge MUST validate `typeof report?.status === 'string'` before returning
Orchestrator healthProbe: existing count(roles) + read agent.state.pendingTasks/roleId + this.schedule no-op + cancelSchedule
NotebookLM + NotebookLM-MCP healthProbe: existing creds check + ping + assert consultNotebook returns NON-EMPTY answer (currently doesn't)
Transcription healthProbe: existing R2/SANDBOX check + Python ping + R2 round-trip (write/read/delete a tiny test object)
Delete the legacy checkOrchestratorAgentRPC / checkNotebookLMAgentRPC / etc. exports — those move into checkHealth(env)
acceptance: All 4 health.ts files export both healthProbe and checkHealth with the documented signatures
checkHealth bridges populate details.probeMs and validate response shape
consultNotebook empty-response test now causes failure

tasks.title: Add @callable() to all 4 agent classes; rename method to healthProbe
actions: Each class: replace `@callable() async checkHealth() { return checkHealth(this, this.env) }` with `@callable() async healthProbe() { return healthProbe(this, this.env) }`
NotebookLMMcpAgent specifically: ADD @callable() — currently missing
Update imports: `import { healthProbe } from './health'`
acceptance: All 4 agents expose @callable() async healthProbe()
NotebookLMMcpAgent now has @callable() (it didn't before)
No agent class still has a checkHealth method

tasks.title: src/backend/api/health.ts (route shape validation via loopback)
actions: Loopback fetch /openapi.json — assert openapi==='3.1.0' and paths non-empty
Loopback fetch /api/ping — assert Content-Type application/json and body { status: 'ok', timestamp: number }
Loopback fetch /api/health/latest — assert NEW { run, results } shape (catches Phase 6 cleanup regressions)
acceptance: All 3 loopback probes pass against the dev server
Wrong Content-Type or wrong shape causes failure with descriptive details

tasks.title: src/backend/api/middleware/health.ts (auth round-trip)
actions: Loopback fetch the auth-protected route WITHOUT Authorization header — assert 401
Same WITH `Authorization: Bearer ${env.WORKER_API_KEY.get()}` — assert 200
Both must pass for status: success
acceptance: Missing-key scenario produces a 401
Valid-key scenario produces a 200
Failure of either produces a clear `details.scenario` indicator

tasks.title: src/backend/health/checks/bindings.ts (5 sub-checks in parallel)
actions:
BROWSER: puppeteer.launch(env.BROWSER) → navigate data:text/html,

Health

→ screenshot → assert PNG bytes > 100 → close. SKIPPED if missing.
R2_AUDIO_BUCKET: put('__health_check', new Uint8Array([1,2,3])) → get → assert bytes match → delete
EMAIL_OUT: assert binding exists; construct + serialize MIMEMessage. NEVER actually send mail.
SESSIONS KV: put('__health_check','1', {expirationTtl:60}) → get → assert === '1' → delete
SANDBOX standalone: provisionSandbox(env, 'binding-health-check') → exec('echo ready') → assert stdout includes 'ready' → destroy
acceptance: 5 sub-checks reported in details, each with status + latency
Disabling any binding (commented out in wrangler.jsonc) yields a single targeted failure

tasks.title: src/backend/health/checks/d1-table-scan.ts
actions: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name NOT LIKE 'sqlite_%'
For each table: SELECT COUNT(*)
Return success with details.warnings: [...] when expected-non-empty tables (roles, documents) are empty
acceptance: Always returns status:success unless the scan itself errors
details.warnings array present and informative when tables are unexpectedly empty

tasks.title: Move secrets / env-vars / notebooklm-credentials / tts / stt into modular files
actions: Move logic from src/backend/services/health.ts:125-228, 383-475 into the corresponding files
TTS: read first chunk of returned ReadableStream, assert byte count > 0
STT: assert result.text !== undefined (key must exist; empty string is OK for silence)
acceptance: TTS no longer accepts a truthy-but-empty stream as success
STT now distinguishes contract drift (missing text key) from quiet input (empty text)

verification: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit → 0 errors after all P3 tasks complete
pnpm run dev boots without runtime errors

phases.name: Coordinator + Hono routes + scheduled handler

tasks.title: Create src/health/coordinator.ts
actions: Class HealthCoordinator(env). Methods: runAllChecks(trigger), getLatestRun()
Use cloudflare-jedi references/health-system.md lines 374-504 as the template
PER_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS=8000, batch health_results inserts at 9 rows/stmt
Imports use tsconfig aliases (@/, @db/, @ai/) — no relative paths
CHECKS array registers all checks from P3 — see PRD §8 for the full import list
acceptance: runAllChecks returns { runId, status, results, durationMs }
Each check is wrapped with the 8s timeout race
D1 inserts batch correctly (no >100 param errors with full result set)

tasks.title: Refactor src/backend/api/routes/health.ts to use HealthCoordinator
actions: Replace runFullScreening / saveScreeningToD1 / getLatestScreening imports with HealthCoordinator
Three routes (paths unchanged): GET /, GET /latest, POST /run
Response shape: { run, results } — uses selectHealthRunSchema and z.array(selectHealthResultSchema) from drizzle-zod
acceptance: All 3 routes return { run, results } shape
OpenAPI spec at /openapi.json reflects new shape automatically (no hardcoded schemas)

tasks.title: Update src/_worker.ts scheduled handler
actions: Replace runFullScreening + saveScreeningToD1 with `new HealthCoordinator(env).runAllChecks('scheduled')` wrapped in ctx.waitUntil
acceptance: curl http://localhost:8787/__scheduled?cron=0+*%2F4+*+*+* triggers a new health_runs row with trigger='scheduled'

verification: curl -X POST http://localhost:8787/api/health/run returns { run, results } with every registered check present
wrangler d1 execute core-resumes --local --command "SELECT * FROM health_runs ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1" returns the latest run
wrangler d1 execute --command "SELECT name, status, duration_ms FROM health_results WHERE run_id = (SELECT id FROM health_runs ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1)" returns one row per registered check

phases.name: Frontend updates

tasks.title: HealthDashboard response-shape migration
actions: Update fetch types to consume { run, results } not { screening: { resultsJson } }
Group results by category (api/database/ai/providers/agents/google/binding/auth/custom)
For Google Drive results: render details.createdDocUrls AND details.folderUrls as clickable links — this is how the user manually verifies styling/rendering
Keep existing skeleton-during-run, copy-to-clipboard, age display, localStorage 'health_screening_complete' event behaviors
acceptance: Dashboard renders the latest run from /api/health/latest on load
Run Now button posts to /api/health/run, shows skeleton, then renders fresh results
Google Drive section shows clickable links to created docs and folders
User can click a Drive doc URL → opens the actual rendered resume in Drive

tasks.title: HealthBadge status enum migration
actions: Read data.run.status (was data.screening.status)
Status enum: healthy | degraded | unhealthy | unknown (was ok | degraded | down | unknown)
Update color map accordingly — emerald / yellow / red / gray
acceptance: Badge color reflects latest run status across all states
Click-through navigates to /health

verification: Load /health locally — page shows results, badge pulses correctly, Drive URLs are clickable
Click a Drive URL — opens a real Doc in Drive that renders the resume template

phases.name: Cleanup

tasks.title: Remove legacy health-screenings schema + table
actions: Delete src/backend/services/health.ts (logic now lives in modular files + coordinator)
Delete src/backend/db/schemas/health-screenings.ts
Remove the corresponding `export *` from src/backend/db/schema.ts
pnpm run db:generate → produces migration that drops health_screenings table
pnpm run db:migrate:local → confirm migration applies
acceptance: tsc --noEmit clean — no consumers reference the deleted module
health_screenings table removed from local D1
Latest health run still works end-to-end after cleanup

tasks.title: Remove ModuleResult shim
actions: Remove `export type ModuleResult = HealthStepResult` shim added in T1.1
acceptance: tsc --noEmit clean

verification: pnpm run dev boots; full health run still passes
wrangler d1 execute --command "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'" no longer lists health_screenings

phases.name: End-to-end verification

tasks.title: Run full verification checklist (PRD §14)
actions: Step through PRD §14 verification list 1-15
Document any failure with the exact health_results.details payload
Confirm Drive doc retention (run twice, only most recent docs remain)
Confirm failure injection works (comment out a binding, only that binding fails)
Confirm details.probeMs populated for all 4 agent results
acceptance: All 15 verification steps pass
Production deploy via pnpm run deploy succeeds
Production /api/health/latest returns the new { run, results } shape

verification: Production health page shows healthy status across all categories with optional providers SKIPPED, not failed
